overview: The Transifex Filter processes online [Transifex](http://www.transifex.org) projects via local `.txp` project files. It connects to a Transifex host, downloads PO resources for translation, processes them as standard filter events, and can push modified translations back. The filter handles credential-based authentication and selective resource processing.

general.openProject.help:
Opens a dialog to view and edit the `.txp` project file before processing begins. This lets you select which resources to include, update credentials, or refresh the resource list from the Transifex server.

If disabled, the filter processes the `.txp` file as-is without prompting.
notes: The project file editor shows read-only source and target locale fields — these are driven by the calling application, not the file itself.

processingNotes: The filter reads a local `.txp` project file containing Transifex host URL, credentials, and resource list.
If the `.txp` file contains no resources, the filter connects to the Transifex host and fetches the full resource list. If resources are already listed, it uses them as-is without refreshing.
For each selected PO resource matching the source locale, the filter downloads the target-language translation file. If no translation exists yet, an empty PO file is created.
Downloaded PO files are processed sequentially, emitting standard filter events identical to those from a PO file.
When using the filter-writer (e.g., in the Filter Events to Raw Document Step), modified PO files are pushed back to Transifex using the same credentials.
limitations: **BETA** — this filter is considered beta quality.

Transifex loses fuzzy flags and their translations when downloading a PO file uploaded with fuzzy entries. To avoid losing target text during round-trips, entries are not labeled as fuzzy — which may cause Transifex to report 100% translated even when translations are not final.
Only PO-based resources in Transifex are supported — TS files are not handled.

Due to pipeline limitations, this filter only works with the **Filter Events to Raw Document Step** for output. It will not work correctly with steps like the Rainbow Translation Kit Creation Step that rewrite output per-resource.

examples.title: Transifex project file (.txp) format
examples.description: The `.txp` input file is a UTF-8 text file describing the Transifex project connection and resource selection. Lines starting with `#` are comments. Each entry can be omitted for defaults. Resource entries without a `yes/no` flag default to selected.
examples.input:
```
host=http://www.transifex.net
user=johndoe
password=xrt34@asf
projectId=myproject
protectApproved=yes
# Fall-back values for the source and target locales:
sourceLocale=en
targetLocale=fr
# Resources
myfile1xmlpo	yes
myfile2odtpo	no
```
